title: orthosiphon glabratus var. parviflorus
intro: Orthosiphon glabratus var. parviflorus, also known as Asian Uva, is a perennial plant native to Southeast Asia, commonly used as a medicinal herb in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The medicinal properties of Orthosiphon glabratus var. parviflorus have been found to help alleviate urinary tract issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from headaches and body aches, making it a popular herbal remedy in Asian cultures. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
